pi_items.source: Task description and standard GeoServer layer naming conventions for reprojected layers
Confirmed in setup_geoserver.sh line 114: table is created with 'ogr2ogr -nln ne_countries' from ne_110m_admin_0_countries.shp
Official EPSG code verified via https://epsg.io/3857 and EPSG database - EPSG:3857 is the authoritative code for Web Mercator (WGS 84 / Pseudo-Mercator)
Title follows standard GeoServer layer naming convention and accurately describes the reprojected dataset

GeoServer REST API documentation and verifier.py lines 85-89 accept both short forms (REPROJECT, FORCE) and full enum names (REPROJECT_TO_DECLARED, FORCE_DECLARED). The actual GeoServer projectionPolicy enum uses the full names per https://docs.geoserver.org/stable/en/user/data/webadmin/layers.html

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the real Natural Earth public dataset (https://www.naturalearthdata.com/), specifically the Admin 0 countries shapefile at 110m scale. The native PostGIS table is named ne_countries (imported from ne_110m_admin_0_countries.shp via ogr2ogr in setup_geoserver.sh line 114). The target reprojected layer should be named ne_countries_3857 with SRS EPSG:3857 (Web Mercator), which is the official EPSG code for Web Mercator projection. The layer title should include 'Web Mercator'. The projection policy should be one of REPROJECT (REPROJECT_TO_DECLARED), FORCE (FORCE_DECLARED), or other valid GeoServer projection handling options.
